claim: The agent harness is the core product that enables agentic coding; without it there are no agents and no agentic engineering
actionable: Focus on building and controlling your agent harness architecture rather than relying on single-instance coding tools like Claude Code
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Harness engineering—building custom agent orchestration systems—is one of the most valuable skills an agentic engineer can learn
actionable: Invest time in learning multi-agent orchestration patterns and building specialized agent teams for your specific domain instead of relying on single models
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Agent teams with memory that track mental models and expertise outperform parallel blank-slate agents because they learn context over time
actionable: Implement agent expertise files and mental models that persist across invocations, allowing agents to build knowledge of the domain and codebase
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Three-tier agent architecture—orchestrators, leads, and workers—enables horizontal scaling while preventing input token growth with multiple agents
actionable: Design agent systems with a single orchestrator layer that delegates to team leads, who then delegate to specialized worker agents to avoid token explosion
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Orchestrator and lead agents should think and delegate work, not execute; workers do the actual implementation in specialized agent teams
actionable: Structure agent harnesses so higher-level agents only prompt and delegate, while specialized workers execute code changes and implement features
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Domain specification in agent system prompts allows agents to operate only on specific parts of the codebase, improving focus and reducing errors
actionable: Define domain boundaries in agent front matter to restrict agents to frontend, backend, database, or other subsystems, preventing cross-domain mistakes
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Agent expertise files (7K+ tokens per agent) track mental models and work history, allowing agents to learn patterns and understand codebase context
actionable: Create expertise files for each agent that accumulate learnings, design decisions, and completed work to improve agent decision-making over time
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Till-done tasks (not to-do or to-done) enforce that agents work until completion rather than stopping at partial progress
actionable: Structure agent task systems to use 'till-done' semantics where agents continue work until all success criteria are met, not just task initiation
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Teaching orchestrators how to prompt other agents increases system coherence because leads and workers inherit the orchestrator's communication style
actionable: Meta-prompt the orchestrator to teach lower-tier agents the correct prompting format, ensuring consistent communication across the entire agent hierarchy
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Building systems that build systems requires focusing on templates and reusable prompts rather than one-off agentic solutions for each problem
actionable: Invest in building agent harnesses and specialized team configurations that can handle classes of problems, not individual tasks
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Using Claude Code as a meta-builder to construct specialized agent harnesses (80% of work) outperforms using Claude Code for direct application building
actionable: Use Claude Code primarily to build and refine your custom agent harness, then deploy that harness for domain-specific work instead of building features directly
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering

claim: Agentic security and threat detection is a massive business opportunity because agents can be exploited at scale if not properly secured
actionable: Build security-first agent systems and consider agentic security as a core product opportunity in the coming years
source_title: My Pi Agent Teams. Claude Code Leak SIGNAL. Harness Engineering
